computation_name: p-Stable LSH for Lp Distance Search
definition: Use p-stable random projections, weighted Lp hashes, or related LSH families to retrieve candidate train neighbors under L1/L2/Lp distances, then compute the exact distance for candidates and convert it to SPECTRA similarity.

scale_profile.best_for: High-dimensional numeric feature vectors where Euclidean, Manhattan, or weighted Lp distance is the scientific axis.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of vectors when hash table count and bucket width are tuned.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Cosine/angular similarity is the true axis; use angular LSH or vector ANN instead.

tunable_parameters.name: p
tunable_parameters.purpose: Selects the Lp norm, commonly p=1 or p=2.

quality_gates: Compare approximate candidates to exact Lp nearest neighbors on a subset.
Report vector scaling, feature weighting, and distance-to-similarity conversion.
Tune bucket width so hard SPECTRA bins retain adequate train-neighbor coverage.
failure_modes: Feature scaling can dominate Lp distances.
Poor bucket width creates either empty buckets or too many candidates.
For high intrinsic dimension, LSH may require many tables for useful recall.
example_usage: python dense_vector_topk_similarity.py --train train.csv --eval eval.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --vector-prefix feat_ --metric euclidean --top-k 50

citations.title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search in lp
citations.evidence: Introduces locality-sensitive hashing algorithms for approximate nearest-neighbor search in Lp spaces.

citations.title: Efficient Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search for Multiple Weighted lp Distance Functions
citations.evidence: Develops WLSH for approximate nearest-neighbor search under multiple weighted Lp distances.
